schema_notes: Per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research + regen_v3@sha256-e229deb7be66d257 + regen_v3@sha256-9e7d4886cb3665d3

person.wealth_source: Alphabet / Google co-founder; large Class B and Class C Alphabet stake; Bayshore Global Management (family office) manages non-Alphabet holdings including Tesla stock
person.biographical_notes: Co-founded Google with Larry Page in 1998. Diagnosed with LRRK2 mutation (Parkinson's risk variant) after 23andMe testing c. 2008 — this is the documented origin of his multibillion-dollar commitment to Parkinson's research. Divorced Anne Wojcicki (23andMe co-founder) in 2015; the jointly-operated Brin Wojcicki Foundation pre-dates the split.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 RTB: $255.6B at Jan 13 2026 (rank #3, after Alphabet hit $4T); Livemint citing Forbes RTB $248.4B late 2025; Times of India reporting Forbes 2026 $238.4B (rank #5). ~7% spread across sources. Alphabet AI rally in 2025 drove a large step-up; ranking and value have been volatile week-to-week.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: Concentrated in Alphabet (public; Brin's stake is ~5-6% of shares outstanding — divestiture moves the stock but less than Ellison's Oracle position). Large Tesla holdings also liquid. Bayshore Global Management holds non-public investments. Higher liquidity than Ellison because stake is smaller relative to total float. Apply 45% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now' calculations.

cited_events.recipient: Multiple (Michael J. Fox Foundation $253M, UCSF $68.1M, Climate Imperative Foundation $49M, Stanford $32M, and others per 990-PF)
cited_events.amount_usd_note: ProPublica summary: charitable disbursements $722.4M; contributions received into the foundation $161.2M in same year. IP reported $698M in a prior version and $900M grants-made in a later version; the $722.4M figure tracks ProPublica 990-PF summary.

cited_events.amount_note: Founding year — vehicle received $268.3M in initial contributions but disbursed no grants that year.

cited_events.dedup_note: Brin Wojcicki Foundation was jointly operated with ex-wife Anne Wojcicki. Per ProPublica, filings exist through FY2019 but post-2015 detail is thin. Attribution between Brin and Wojcicki is unclear post-2015 (divorce 2015); in strict accounting this should be split 50/50, but since Anne is separately tracked and has her own Giving Pledge (2022), this record counts BWF fully under Brin for rollup purposes with note.

cited_events.amount_note: 2022 total expenses ~$13.1M; ~$13M on climate change, ~$78K on neurological research. Notable recipients: Sixteen Thirty Fund $7M, Energy Action Fund $5.7M.
Inside Philanthropy report: ~$22M climate, balance across CNS disease and other causes. Separately Inside Philanthropy reports $44M in total Catalyst4 grants in 2024.
cited_events.dedup_note: 501(c)(4) not 501(c)(3). Under IPS/Forbes/Chronicle methodology 501(c)(4)s count for social welfare but are distinct from traditional charitable giving because they can lobby and political-advocate. Flag for reclassification review.

cited_events.recipient: Sergey Brin Family Foundation and/or Catalyst4 (exact split not disclosed)
cited_events.dedup_note: This is a transfer INTO Brin's foundations. Per dedup rule #2, count foundation grants OUT — not transfers IN — to avoid double counting. The foundation will disburse these funds in future years and they will then appear as grants_out.

cited_events.amount_note: ~$100M each in May 2024 and December 2024, per SEC Form 4 filings reported by Bloomberg/PND.
cited_events.dedup_note: Transfer IN — matched against SBFF 2024 contributions_received $161.2M on ProPublica; press $200M vs. 990 $161.2M suggests part landed in Catalyst4 or across fiscal-year boundary.

cited_events.recipient: Catalyst4 (~80% of shares) + Sergey Brin Family Foundation
cited_events.dedup_note: Transfer IN. Will flow OUT in subsequent years and be captured then as grants_out.

cited_events.recipient: Primary: Catalyst4 (~$1B); Sergey Brin Family Foundation (~$90M); Michael J. Fox Foundation direct (~$45M)
cited_events.amount_note: 1,778,650 Class A + 1,778,650 Class C Alphabet shares = ~3.56M shares per SEC Form 4. Valued at ~$1.1B based on market price. Recipient allocations disclosed by Brin's family-office spokesperson.
cited_events.dedup_note: The $45M direct gift to Michael J. Fox Foundation IS a direct_gift (counts as grant_out). The $1B to Catalyst4 and $90M to SBFF are transfers_in. Split event when rolling up.

cited_events.recipient: Michael J. Fox Foundation for Parkinson's Research
cited_events.dedup_note: Direct personal gift to 501(c)(3) — counts as grant_out under dedup rules. Separated from the Catalyst4/SBFF portions of the same Nov 2025 transaction.

cited_events.recipient: Parkinson's disease research — primarily Michael J. Fox Foundation, Aligning Science Across Parkinson's (ASAP), and affiliated grantees
cited_events.amount_usd_note: Forbes Feb 2025 ('more than $1.5B'). Earlier Forbes Dec 2022 cited '$1B+'. Philanthropy News Digest in 2024 cited '$1.1B'. By late 2025 press: closer to $1.5-2B. This is a cross-vehicle aggregate claim, not a discrete event — used for narrative, NOT additive to observable_giving rollup (the underlying grants are captured by SBFF/BWF/Catalyst4 990 rows and the direct-gift rows above).
cited_events.dedup_note: DO NOT ADD TO ROLLUP. Aggregate narrative only — the constituent grants are already in SBFF/BWF/Catalyst4/direct-gift rows.

pledges_and_announcements.notes: Brin has NOT signed the Giving Pledge. Multiple sources confirm (CNBC 2022, Vox 2019, CCN, IBTimes). Ex-wife Anne Wojcicki signed the Giving Pledge in 2022 on her own; she is listed on givingpledge.org but Sergey is not. Brin has not publicly spoken about the Giving Pledge.

Listed for context only. Anne Wojcicki is not the subject of this record; her pledge does NOT encumber Sergey's assets. If BWF survives under joint control (ProPublica shows ambiguous post-divorce status), pledge dollars might come out of its corpus — but that's Anne's commitment, not Sergey's.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Brin's political giving is more issue-oriented (housing, anti-wealth-tax ballot measures, climate-adjacent) than party-machine.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Rough floor: $20M to California housing / ballot-measure political effort (NYT Jan 2026), $78,400 max-out to Matt Mahan for California governor (Guardian Mar 2026), plus pre-2020 smaller FEC-level giving to Democratic candidates/DNC per OpenSecrets. FEC API pull not yet run.

sources.note: $20M to California political drive on housing — described as 'may also help ward off a wealth tax.'
Max-out to Matt Mahan (Dem) for California governor.
Historical Democratic giving (Obama, DNC, clean-energy ballot measures) per OpenSecrets; no presidential endorsement 2024.

foundations_active.name: Sergey Brin Family Foundation
foundations_active.fiscal_agent: Pacific Foundation Services (1660 Bush St. No. 300, San Francisco, CA 94109)
officers: Tara Farnsworth (Treasurer, also Bayshore Global Management CFO)
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (all officers zero compensation across all filed years 2014-2024)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 payout $722.4M / $4.31B = ~16.8% — well above 5% legal minimum; no LOW_PAYOUT flag. Multi-year trend: payout has accelerated sharply from $2.5M in 2015 (1st full grantmaking year) to $722M in 2024.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — ProPublica summary does not disclose individual grant recipients; would need to parse full 990-PF schedules.

foundations_active.name: Catalyst4 Inc.
foundations_active.reported_legal_address_note: Inside Philanthropy reports Catalyst4 was 'legally headquartered in a UPS store' — consistent with lean-vehicle approach.
officers: Ekemini Riley (Board Member — also co-leads ASAP)
foundations_active.seeded_with_note: Initial $366M of Tesla-stock-sale proceeds in 2021 (per InfluenceWatch citing Candid).
foundations_active.tax_status_note: 501(c)(4) — social welfare, CAN engage in unlimited lobbying and some political activity. Under IPS/Forbes/Chronicle methodology this is distinct from 501(c)(3) charitable giving. We include Catalyst4 in observable_giving but flag that ~$44M/yr in 501(c)(4) expenditures is not the same category as SBFF charity.

foundations_uncertain_status.name: The Brin Wojcicki Foundation
foundations_uncertain_status.status: UNCERTAIN — spokesperson claims active, filings through 2019, not listed on IRS most-recent tax-exempt organizations list per InfluenceWatch
foundations_uncertain_status.joint_control_note: Jointly operated by Sergey Brin and Anne Wojcicki. Divorce finalized 2015. Anne's IP spokesperson (2018) said BWF 'is still in operation.' Post-2015 financial detail on ProPublica is thin. Attribution of post-2015 giving between Sergey and Anne is unclear.

operating_initiatives_funded.name: Aligning Science Across Parkinson's (ASAP)
operating_initiatives_funded.implementation_partner: Michael J. Fox Foundation for Parkinson's Research
operating_initiatives_funded.leadership: Ekemini Riley (Managing Director; also Catalyst4 board), Randy Schekman (Nobel laureate, scientific director)
operating_initiatives_funded.granting_activity: Multiple grant rounds totaling $132M (three-year initial) and later $161M first round per PND reports. Recent 2025 rounds offer up to $6M per team over 3 years.
operating_initiatives_funded.classification_note: NOT a separate 501(c)(3) — implemented through MJFF grants from SBFF. Funds flow: Brin → SBFF → MJFF/ASAP → researchers. The $253M 2024 MJFF grant from SBFF is the dominant vector.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only. Assume unknown lower bound; see hidden_upper calculation.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Bayshore Global Management is Brin's family office — explicitly a personal/financial management entity, NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective. No known philanthropic LLC for Brin.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.
detected_vehicles.trusts_note: No publicly disclosed personal trust of the kind Ellison's pledge letter references. Brin's non-charitable wealth management runs through Bayshore Global; specific estate-planning structure not public.

red_flags.evidence: Multiple sources confirm Brin has never signed the Giving Pledge and has not publicly spoken about it. Ex-wife Anne Wojcicki signed separately in 2022.
red_flags.severity_note: Weaker signal than PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED — Brin never made the commitment publicly, so there's no breach. But given net worth and 22 years as a billionaire, absence of pledge is itself notable.

red_flags.evidence: NYT Jan 2026: $20M to California political drive whose aims 'may also help ward off a wealth tax.' Structurally concerning: political deployment to protect personal wealth reduces the potential philanthropic pool.

Catalyst4 received $1B+ of Brin's 2025 Alphabet stock gifts vs. $90M to SBFF — Brin is routing his marginal giving through a 501(c)(4) advocacy vehicle rather than a 501(c)(3) charity. 501(c)(4) allows unlimited lobbying and greater opacity. Still deductible to donor for some purposes but a different instrument than traditional charity.

Between 2014 (SBFF established with $268M) and 2020, SBFF disbursed less than $500M cumulative while Brin's net worth grew from ~$30B to $80B+. Giving only accelerated to current $700M/yr level after 2022. Pre-2020 'giving rate' was <0.3% of net worth annually.

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (all SBFF and Catalyst4 officers zero comp)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified; not confirmed absent)
PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (never signed — flagged separately as NO_GIVING_PLEDGE_SIGNATURE)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(SBFF 990-PF grants_out 2014-2024) + sum(BWF 990-PF grants_out 2011-2015) + Catalyst4 2022 ($13M) + Catalyst4 2024 ($44M, press) + MJFF Nov 2025 direct gift ($45M) + HIAS 2009 ($1M). Excludes: stock gift TRANSFERS into foundations (dedup rule #2); the Parkinson's lifetime aggregate narrative claim. SBFF total 1997-2024: ~$2.379B. BWF 2011-2015: ~$116M. Catalyst4: ~$57M. Direct gifts: ~$46M. Grand total ~$2.44B.
observable_giving_todo: Pull BWF 990-PF filings for years 2016-2019 — ProPublica has the filings but key fields were not in summary; parse XML to get grants_paid.
Split BWF post-divorce giving between Brin and Wojcicki (attribution unclear).
Pull SBFF 2024 full 990-PF (not just summary) to verify whether $722M includes $45M MJFF direct gift (which would then need to be subtracted to avoid double-count).
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 G-transactions for Brin's CIK to identify any stock gifts not routed through SBFF/Catalyst4 (e.g., direct to operating charities).
Verify Catalyst4 2023 990 when available (only 2021-2022 currently summarized).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.45 × $248.4B = $111.78B; tenure cap saturated (22 years). Expected = $11.178B. Higher liquidity factor than Ellison (0.45 vs 0.25) because Brin's Alphabet stake is a smaller fraction of float.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.c4_uncounted_method: Catalyst4 received $1B+ of transfers in 2025; most not yet disbursed. If/when disbursed as climate/CNS advocacy (501(c)(4)), Forbes/Chronicle methodology may partially credit. Set uncertainty envelope of ~$500M as plausible near-term disbursement not yet appearing in 990s.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Brin is documented as historically preferring relative privacy (Forbes Dec 2022: 'quietly' became largest Parkinson's donor). Apply 1% of liquid_wealth ceiling × 0.22 probability weight = $111.78B × 0.01 × 0.22 = $246M. Round to $250M.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Brin has given to HIAS (Hebrew Immigrant Aid Society, $1M 2009) but is not reported to tithe at scale. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.parkinsons_pledge_future_note: Brin has not made a quantified future pledge. Future giving will be captured by 990s as it occurs.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Brin: 0.286, so fails strict Verified_Low. Probably_Low because: (a) observable giving of $2.44B is real and documented, (b) giving rate has accelerated sharply since 2022 ($722M/yr run-rate), (c) Parkinson's commitment is specific and demonstrated, (d) 2025 stock transfers of ~$1.9B signal more is coming. But 22 years of compounding wealth with <1% lifetime giving-to-current-NW ratio, no Giving Pledge, and increasing channeling through 501(c)(4) advocacy keeps him in Tier B rather than Tier C/D.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Brin is the canonical case of 'real giving but still small relative to the pile.' Observable ~$2.4B is ~1% of current net worth and ~22% of the 10% formal norm. His Parkinson's commitment is genuine and historically significant — he is, factually, the largest individual donor to Parkinson's research ever. But that commitment now represents a tiny fraction of his wealth. Trajectory matters: if 2025's $2B in stock transfers disburses on 2024's $722M/yr cadence, his ratio will improve meaningfully in coming years.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity. Pledge-breach = 0 because he never signed (no pledge to breach). Giving-gap = (1 - 0.218) × 40 = 31.3. Tenure saturated at 22 years. Red flags saturated (4 present). DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.
